photojournalism.newsExtreme.title: News extreme
photojournalism.newsExtreme.description: Catastrophic, world-altering, and extreme global news events and disasters
examples: a high-profile billionaire is arrested on live television for running a global espionage ring
a massive hurricane hits a major city, flooding streets and homes
a massive cyber attack that shuts down a major city's power grid
a major political scandal threatens a major leader's career
the first human brain-computer interface is successfully implanted, allowing telepathic communication
a major corporation is caught in a massive bribery scandal

photojournalism.newsNormal.title: News normal
photojournalism.newsNormal.description: Standard major news headlines covering science, politics, business, and society
examples: a major business scandal, such as a ponzi scheme or insider trading
a major cultural festival, celebrating the traditions and customs of a particular group or region
a major technology conference, bringing together innovators and entrepreneurs from around the world
a major educational development, such as a new policy or program aimed at improving student outcomes
a major art exhibition, featuring groundbreaking works by contemporary artists
a major international airport suffers a system-wide it failure, grounding hundreds of commercial flights

photojournalism.newsSpecific.title: News specific
photojournalism.newsSpecific.description: Highly detailed, specific descriptions of major global news events and crises
examples: a major civil rights movement: the black lives matter movement gains widespread support and leads to major policy changes, including police reform and the removal of confederate statues
a mass shooting or act of domestic terrorism: a gunman opens fire at a crowded concert in las vegas, killing dozens and injuring hundreds more
a historic space launch: the world's most powerful rocket lifts off from cape canaveral, carrying a new space telescope to study the early universe
a technological breakthrough: scientists create a new type of battery that is both more efficient and less expensive than existing technologies, revolutionizing the energy industry
a critical space rescue: an international crew of astronauts executes a daring spacewalk to repair a damaged life-support system
a new scientific discovery: scientists discover a new species of dinosaur, providing new insights into the prehistoric world and sparking excitement among paleontologists and the public alike

photojournalism.styleProtest.title: Style protest
photojournalism.styleProtest.description: Various photographic styles and techniques used in photojournalism and documentary photography
examples: color photography: capturing the vibrancy and energy of protests using bold and vivid colors
tintype and wet plate photography: employing historic nineteenth-century processes to produce highly textured, hauntingly historicized portraits
close-up portraiture: focusing tightly on the expressive faces of individual protesters to highlight intense human emotion and determination
intentional camera movement: deliberately moving the camera during a longer exposure to create a dynamic, abstract representation of chaos and action
creative lighting photography: using creative lighting techniques to add drama and impact to protest images
environmental portraiture: taking portraits of the protesters and the people affected by the issues being addressed

photojournalism.types.title: Types of photojournalism
photojournalism.types.description: Diverse subjects and thematic beats covered in photojournalism and documentary reporting
examples: photojournalistic documentation of the impact of gentrification and urban development on communities
coverage of human rights abuses and violations
advocacy photojournalism, documenting specific causes to raise awareness and inspire social change

set.moviegenBench.title: Benchmark prompt set from MovieGenBench
set.moviegenBench.description: MovieGen Video Bench contains 1003 diverse prompts testing various aspects including human activities (motion, emotions), animals, nature/scenery, physics (fluids, gravity, collisions), and unusual subjects/activities. The prompts provide comprehensive coverage across high, medium and low motion scenarios.
examples: A person playing a harp strung with rainbows, creating music that colors the air.
An empty warehouse dynamically transformed by flora that explode from the ground.
A dramatic scene of two cars colliding at an intersection, with shattered glass and debris flying in the air, capturing the intensity and impact of the crash.
A white cat driving in a car through a busy downtown street with tall buildings and pedestrians in the background
A woman sipping a steaming cup of tea.
A coconut tree made of dollar bills at sunset, with bills falling off like leaves.

set.parti.title: PartiPrompts
set.parti.description: PartiPrompts (P2) is a rich set of over 1600 prompts in English. P2 can be used to measure model capabilities across various categories and challenge aspects.

a red train is coming down the beach
an abstract painting of three triangles in blue, yellow and red
Portrait of a gecko wearing a train conductor’s hat and holding a flag that has a yin-yang symbol on it. Marble statue.
